definitions.definition: Designating the side of the body which is positioned to the east if one is facing north, the side on which the heart is not located in most humans. This arrow points to the reader's right: →

parsedExamples.example: After the accident, her right leg was slightly shorter than her left.

examples: After the accident, her right leg was slightly shorter than her left.

definitions.definition: Clockwise, particularly when describing a change in direction or orientation.

parsedExamples.example: The road up ahead contains a right bend.
Rotate the bolt to the right to tighten it.

examples: The road up ahead contains a right bend.
Rotate the bolt to the right to tighten it.

definitions.definition: Complying with justice, correctness, or reason; correct, just, true. See also the interjection senses below.

parsedExamples.example: That's not the right thing to do.
So I was right all along? C'mon. I want to hear you say it.

examples: That's not the right thing to do.
So I was right all along? C'mon. I want to hear you say it.

definitions.definition: Appropriate, perfectly suitable; fit for purpose.

parsedExamples.example: Is this the right software for my computer?

examples: Is this the right software for my computer?

definitions.definition: Healthy, sane, competent.

parsedExamples.example: I'm afraid my father is no longer in his right mind.

examples: I'm afraid my father is no longer in his right mind.

definitions.definition: Real; veritable (used emphatically).

parsedExamples.example: You've made a right mess of the kitchen!

examples: You've made a right mess of the kitchen!

definitions.definition: Of an angle, measuring 90 degrees, or one quarter of a complete rotation; the angle between two perpendicular lines.

parsedExamples.example: The kitchen counter formed a right angle with the back wall.

examples: The kitchen counter formed a right angle with the back wall.

definitions.definition: Of a geometric figure, incorporating a right angle between edges, faces, axes, etc.

parsedExamples.example: a right triangle   a right prism   a right cone

examples: a right triangle   a right prism   a right cone

definitions.definition: Designating the bank of a river (etc.) on one's right when facing downstream (i.e. facing forward while floating with the current); that is, the south bank of a river that flows eastward. If this arrow: ⥴ shows the direction of the current, the tilde is on the right side of the river.

parsedExamples.example: The Louvre Museum is on the right bank of the Seine.

examples: The Louvre Museum is on the right bank of the Seine.

definitions.definition: Designed to be placed or worn outward.

parsedExamples.example: the right side of a piece of cloth   Begin this stitch on the right side.

examples: the right side of a piece of cloth   Begin this stitch on the right side.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to the political right; conservative.
All right; not requiring assistance.
Most favourable or convenient; fortunate.
Straight, not bent.

parsedExamples.example: a right line

examples: a right line

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the right whale.
On the right side.
Towards the right side.
Exactly, precisely.

parsedExamples.example: The arrow landed right in the middle of the target.
Luckily we arrived right at the start of the film.

examples: The arrow landed right in the middle of the target.
Luckily we arrived right at the start of the film.

definitions.definition: Immediately, directly.

parsedExamples.example: Can't you see it? It's right beside you!
Tom was standing right in front of the TV, blocking everyone's view.
You come right home this instant!

examples: Can't you see it? It's right beside you!
Tom was standing right in front of the TV, blocking everyone's view.
You come right home this instant!

definitions.definition: Very, extremely, quite.

parsedExamples.example: I made a right stupid mistake there, didn't I?
I stubbed my toe a week ago and it still hurts right much.
Sir, I am right glad to meet you …
Members of the Queen's Privy Council are styled The Right Honourable for life.
The Right Reverend Monsignor Guido Sarducci.

examples: I made a right stupid mistake there, didn't I?
I stubbed my toe a week ago and it still hurts right much.
Sir, I am right glad to meet you …
Members of the Queen's Privy Council are styled The Right Honourable for life.
The Right Reverend Monsignor Guido Sarducci.

definitions.definition: To a great extent or degree.

parsedExamples.example: Sir, I am right glad to meet you …
Members of the Queen's Privy Council are styled The Right Honourable for life.
The Right Reverend Monsignor Guido Sarducci.

examples: Sir, I am right glad to meet you …
Members of the Queen's Privy Council are styled The Right Honourable for life.
The Right Reverend Monsignor Guido Sarducci.

definitions.definition: According to fact or truth; actually; truly; really.
In a correct manner.

parsedExamples.example: Do it right or don't do it at all.
Nothing is going right for me lately.

examples: Do it right or don't do it at all.
Nothing is going right for me lately.

definitions.definition: Yes, that is correct; I agree.

parsedExamples.example:
— United's the best team in the country.
— Right. And they'll go all the way for sure.
— Damn right they will.

examples:
— United's the best team in the country.
— Right. And they'll go all the way for sure.
— Damn right they will.

definitions.definition: I have listened to what you just said and I acknowledge your assertion or opinion, regardless of whether I agree with it (opinion) or can verify it (assertion).

parsedExamples.example:
— United's the best team in the country, so they'll come up with something.
— Right. And do you think they'll go all the way?

examples:
— United's the best team in the country, so they'll come up with something.
— Right. And do you think they'll go all the way?

definitions.definition: Signpost word to change the subject in a discussion or discourse.

parsedExamples.example:
— After that interview, I don't think we should hire her.
— Right. Who wants lunch?

examples:
— After that interview, I don't think we should hire her.
— Right. Who wants lunch?

definitions.definition: Used to check listener engagement and (especially) agreement at the end of an utterance or each segment thereof.

parsedExamples.example: You're going, right?
I went downstairs, right, and I was going to call her, but I found this note, right, so what am I supposed to do now?

examples: You're going, right?
I went downstairs, right, and I was going to call her, but I found this note, right, so what am I supposed to do now?

definitions.definition: Used to add seriousness or decisiveness before a statement.
That which complies with justice, law or reason.

parsedExamples.example: We're on the side of right in this contest.

examples: We're on the side of right in this contest.

definitions.definition: A legal, just or moral entitlement.

parsedExamples.example: You have no right to go through my personal diary.

examples: You have no right to go through my personal diary.

definitions.definition: The right side or direction.

parsedExamples.example: The pharmacy is just on the right past the bookshop.

examples: The pharmacy is just on the right past the bookshop.

definitions.definition: The right hand or fist.
The authority to perform, publish, film, or televise a particular work, event, etc.; a copyright.
The ensemble of right-wing political parties; political conservatives as a group.

parsedExamples.example: The political right holds too much power.

examples: The political right holds too much power.

definitions.definition: The outward or most finished surface, as of a coin, piece of cloth, a carpet, etc.
A wave breaking from right to left (viewed from the shore).
To correct.

parsedExamples.example: Righting all the wrongs of the war immediately will be impossible.

examples: Righting all the wrongs of the war immediately will be impossible.

definitions.definition: To set upright.

parsedExamples.example: The tow-truck righted what was left of the automobile.

examples: The tow-truck righted what was left of the automobile.

definitions.definition: To return to normal upright position.

parsedExamples.example: When the wind died down, the ship righted.

examples: When the wind died down, the ship righted.

definitions.definition: To do justice to; to relieve from wrong; to restore rights to; to assert or regain the rights of.

parsedExamples.example: to right the oppressed

examples: to right the oppressed

definitions.definition: A good deed; a right action.
A just or equitable action.
A law, ruling, judgement or rule.
A right, entitlement or privilege.
Truth, correctness.
right (direction; as opposed to the left)
Straight; not crooked or bent.
On the or at the right (as opposed to left)
Morally or legally correct or justified.
Real, genuine, authentic, true.
Natural, undisturbed.
right fielder
